project.name: vim-rogue
project.description: A roguelike dungeon game with ASCII aesthetic in a graphical window, teaching Vim motions through gameplay

nodes.name: main.rs
nodes.summary: Binary entry point that initializes bracket-lib console, sets up the main event loop, and delegates to game tick and renderer.
nodes.languageNotes: Thin wrapper using bracket-lib's BTermBuilder and main_loop pattern.

nodes.name: game.rs
nodes.summary: App coordinator implementing core game logic: handle_key for input processing, tick for animation updates, execute_motion for player movement, enemies_step for enemy turn orchestration, and level transitions.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains impl App with 18 methods including tick (74 lines), handle_key (121 lines), enemies_step (95 lines), and execute_motion (61 lines).

nodes.name: player.rs
nodes.summary: PlayerState implementation with all 13 Vim motion handlers (h/j/k/l/w/b/0/$/G/gg/f/t/dd), boundary checking, wall stopping logic, and motion tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: impl PlayerState with handle_motion as primary entry (55 lines), plus step, jump_word_forward/backward, find_char, till_char.

nodes.name: map.rs
nodes.summary: 80x40 grid map with 5 zones, 4 dungeon levels with procedural generation (carve_level, build_level_2/3/4), corridor carving, enemy spawn points, patrol areas, and torchlight placement.
nodes.languageNotes: impl Map with is_passable, zone_at, carve_level (96 lines), build_level_2/3/4 variants, and room-based generation.

nodes.name: renderer.rs
nodes.summary: Pure rendering module using bracket-lib to draw title/gameplay/win/lost/pause screens, viewport with FOV fog, sidebar with stats, minimap, zone-colored walls, and animation effects.
nodes.languageNotes: 30+ functions with render (37 lines), render_map_viewport (113 lines), render_sidebar (123 lines), render_title (101 lines). Read-only access to app state.

nodes.name: animation.rs
nodes.summary: Animation system with GameClock trait (RealClock/TestClock), AnimationState for smooth transitions, AnimationTimer for duration tracking, Interpolator for easing functions, and AttackEffect for combat visuals.
nodes.languageNotes: Trait-based clock abstraction enables deterministic testing. Constants: PLAYER_MOVE_MS (150), ENEMY_MOVE_MS (200).

nodes.name: visibility.rs
nodes.summary: FOV (field of view) system using ray-casting to compute visible tiles from player position, with three visibility states (Hidden/Explored/Visible) that persist explored areas.
nodes.languageNotes: VisibilityMap owns 2D grid of VisibilityState. compute_fov ray-casts in all directions with FOV_RADIUS (10).

nodes.name: enemy.rs
nodes.summary: Enemy AI with FOV-aware BFS pathfinding for chase (step_toward_player), Bresenham line-of-sight check, and patrol movement within assigned PatrolArea when player not visible.
nodes.languageNotes: impl Enemy with step_toward_player (52 lines BFS), has_line_of_sight (Bresenham), patrol_step. ENEMY_FOV_RADIUS = 8.

nodes.name: audio.rs
nodes.summary: Audio manager with SoundEffect enum for all game events, enable/disable toggle, and graceful no-op fallback when audio is disabled (default state).
nodes.languageNotes: No-op implementation by default. AudioManager::enable() activates playback.

nodes.name: test_support.rs
nodes.summary: Test seam providing App::for_test constructor that creates an App with a custom map and position, used by all 396 integration tests for deterministic setup.
nodes.languageNotes: Single impl App with #[doc(hidden)] for_test. Sets session.started = true and updates visibility.

nodes.name: spike.rs
nodes.summary: Proof-of-concept spike demonstrating bracket-lib basics: console creation, character drawing, background colors, and input handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Standalone example, not part of main build. Demonstrates BTermBuilder pattern used in main.rs.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Shared test helpers providing test_map for blank map creation, tick_timer/tick_state for animation testing, approx_eq for float comparison, and all_transparent/with_walls for FOV testing.
nodes.languageNotes: Common utilities imported by all 9 test files. Some functions trigger dead_code warnings when not used by subset.

nodes.name: game.rs
nodes.summary: 140 comprehensive integration tests for game state machine: all 13 motions, pending input (f/t/dd/gg), animation queueing, level transitions, enemy collisions, audio, trail tracking, visibility updates, win/loss/retry flows, pause menu, and melee combat.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest test file (1968 lines). Uses App::for_test extensively. Tests handle_key, tick, execute_motion, enemies_step.

nodes.name: player.rs
nodes.summary: 32 tests for all 13 Vim motions (h/j/k/l/w/b/0/$/G/gg/f/t/dd) including boundary checks, wall stopping for word jumps, motion recording, motion_count increment, and discovered_motions set tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: Tests handle_key for each motion. Verifies position changes and PlayerState mutations.

nodes.name: map.rs
nodes.summary: 46 tests for map structure: dimensions (80x40), tile types, passability rules, 5 zone assignments, corridor connectivity, obstacle placement, all 4 level layouts, reachability from start to exit, enemy spawn points, patrol areas, and torchlight room presence.
nodes.languageNotes: Tests Map::level(1-4) generation. Verifies grid contents with get_tile and zone_at.

nodes.name: renderer.rs
nodes.summary: 53 tests for rendering: zone color mapping, wall glyph selection, duration formatting, animation phase definitions, exit glow animation, trail color gradients, minimap rendering, fog appearance, viewport centering, and pub-exposed internal functions.
nodes.languageNotes: Tests pure rendering functions with sample App states. Verifies colors, glyphs, and layout calculations.

nodes.name: animation.rs
nodes.summary: 34 tests for animation system: AnimationTimer progress tracking, interpolation functions (linear, ease-in, ease-out, ease-in-out), AnimationState transitions, TestClock determinism, and AttackEffect rendering.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses TestClock for deterministic timing. Tests tick_timer and tick_state helpers from common/mod.rs.

nodes.name: visibility.rs
nodes.summary: 29 tests for FOV system: center position visibility, wall blocking, radius bounds, explored state persistence across updates, reset behavior, corridor visibility, symmetry from player position, and multi-source torchlight FOV.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses all_transparent, with_walls, with_transparent_tiles helpers. Tests VisibilityMap and compute_fov.

nodes.name: enemy.rs
nodes.summary: 21 tests for enemy AI: BFS pathfinding toward player, diagonal movement, wall avoidance, adjacency checks, corridor following, shortest path selection, line-of-sight detection, and patrol area movement.
nodes.languageNotes: Tests step_toward_player (BFS), has_line_of_sight (Bresenham), and patrol_step with PatrolArea bounds.

nodes.name: audio.rs
nodes.summary: 16 tests for audio system: AudioManager lifecycle (new, enable, disable), play no-op behavior when disabled, rapid play sequencing, and all SoundEffect enum variants.
nodes.languageNotes: Verifies audio is disabled by default. Tests enable/disable toggle and play method behavior.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Game coordinator with 18 methods including new (constructor), tick (animation updates), handle_key (input processing), execute_motion (movement), enemies_step (AI orchestration), and level transition helpers.

nodes.name: tick
nodes.summary: Main game loop update that processes animations, advances enemy turns when player animation complete, checks win/loss conditions, handles checkpoint respawn, and updates visibility.

nodes.name: handle_key
nodes.summary: Input handler that processes all keypresses: Vim motions, pause menu (ESC/q), navigation (j/k/arrows), melee attack (x), and debug cheats, delegating to execute_motion or modifying state directly.

nodes.name: enemies_step
nodes.summary: Orchestrates enemy turn: calls World::step_enemies to get movements, processes collisions with player (damage, stun, death), pushes enemies to avoid overlapping, and plays audio/sound effects.

nodes.name: execute_motion
nodes.summary: Executes Vim motion by calling PlayerState::handle_motion, checking exit/win condition, queuing player animation, advancing enemy turn, checking wall collisions, and updating trail.

nodes.name: handle_motion
nodes.summary: Vim motion dispatcher that records the motion, updates motion tracking, and dispatches to specific motion implementations (step, jump_word_forward/backward, find_char, till_char, delete_obstacle, jump_to_column).

nodes.name: step
nodes.summary: Single-tile movement handler for h/j/k/l that checks bounds, verifies passability with can_pass_to, updates position, and returns success status.

nodes.name: render
nodes.summary: Main render dispatcher that selects screen (Title/Gameplay/Win/Lost), checks minimum screen size, renders resize notice if needed, and delegates to specific render functions.

nodes.name: render_map_viewport
nodes.summary: Renders the 80x40 map viewport with FOV fog, zone-colored walls and floors, player trail, animated player and enemies, exit glow, and attack effects, centering on player position.

nodes.name: render_sidebar
nodes.summary: Renders the right sidebar with zone title, 13 Vim motion descriptions (highlighting discovered ones), HP bar (Level 4), elapsed time, motion mastery progress, and minimap.

nodes.name: step_toward_player
nodes.summary: BFS pathfinding that computes shortest path to player, checks line of sight, moves one step along path if visible, or falls back to patrol movement if player hidden.

nodes.name: has_line_of_sight
nodes.summary: Bresenham line algorithm that checks if all tiles between enemy and player are transparent (no walls), returning true if player is visible.

nodes.name: compute_fov
nodes.summary: Ray-casting FOV algorithm that casts rays in all directions from center position, stops at walls, and marks visible tiles within FOV_RADIUS.

nodes.name: App::for_test
nodes.summary: Test-only constructor that creates an App with custom map and player position, sets session.started = true, computes initial visibility, and provides deterministic test setup.

nodes.name: test_map
nodes.summary: Test helper that creates a blank map of specified dimensions filled with Floor tiles in Zone1, with no enemies or obstacles.

nodes.name: .git-blame-ignore-revs
nodes.summary: Git blame ignore configuration for excluding specific commits from blame annotations (3 lines).

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow running format check, clippy linting, testing across 6 platforms, and cross-compilation builds.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-platform matrix builds for Linux, macOS, Windows (x86_64 and ARM64) using cross-rs for Linux targets.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions release workflow triggered on version tags, building cross-platform binaries and publishing GitHub releases with auto-extracted changelog.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses cross-rs for Linux ARM64/x86_64 cross-compilation; extracts versioned changelog sections with sed; uploads platform-specific tar.gz/zip archives.

nodes.name: AGENTS.original.md
nodes.summary: Original root-level documentation before agent skill integration, containing structure and architecture references.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project overview with architecture, conventions, commands, verification checklist, dependencies, and agent skill configuration for AI-assisted development.

nodes.name: CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Domain context document defining aggregates (World, PlayerState, InputState, Session, App), domain rules, and naming conventions for DDD-style architecture.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Rust package manifest for vim-rogue 0.2.2 with edition 2024 and dependencies on bracket-lib 0.8.7 (unused anyhow listed).

nodes.name: Cross.toml
nodes.summary: Cross-rs configuration for cross-compilation targeting Linux x86_64 and ARM64 with environment passthrough.

nodes.name: domain.md
nodes.summary: Agent workflow instructions for domain documentation exploration emphasizing glossary usage, file structure understanding, and ADR conflict detection.

nodes.name: issue-tracker.md
nodes.summary: GitHub Issues workflow conventions via gh CLI for agent skills with title formatting, label usage, and CRUD operation patterns.

nodes.name: triage-labels.md
nodes.summary: Five canonical triage role labels for agent-based issue triage workflow with default naming conventions.

nodes.name: aggregate-refactor.md
nodes.summary: Detailed 4-step refactoring specification to split App monolith into domain aggregates (World, PlayerState, InputState, Session) with test impact analysis.

nodes.name: rustfmt.toml
nodes.summary: Rust formatter configuration using small heuristics with Rust edition 2024.

nodes.name: AGENTS.original.md
nodes.summary: Original source directory documentation before agent skill integration with key files and conventions.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Source directory AI assistance guide with key files, where-to-look reference, internal dependencies, conventions, and test patterns.

nodes.name: AGENTS.original.md
nodes.summary: Original tests directory documentation with test file listing and conventions.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Tests directory AI assistance guide with test constructor (App::for_test), shared helpers, and testing conventions.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Module barrel file re-exporting all game modules including the new render_types module. Public API entry point for the library.

nodes.name: types.rs
nodes.summary: Core domain types and aggregates defining Position, Tile, Zone, VimMotion, Direction, GameState, enemy types, and three key aggregates: World (enemies + visibility + map), PlayerState (position + motion tracking + checkpoint), InputState (input queue), and Session (game state + timing). Extracts all renderer-specific types to render_types.rs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses DDD aggregate pattern with three primary aggregates (World, PlayerState, Session) that own their domains and coordinate through App.

nodes.name: render_types.rs
nodes.summary: Renderer-only types extracted from types.rs. Defines RenderCell (glyph + colors + blink), RenderGrid (2D grid with width/height), ScreenModel (Title/Gameplay/Win/Lost states), and ViewModel (screen + frame counter). Used by renderer.rs and tests/types.rs.
nodes.languageNotes: Newly extracted module to separate renderer concerns from domain logic. Renderer uses these types via App, not direct imports.

nodes.name: types.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests for domain types (Tile, VimMotion, Direction, Position, Zone, Enemy) and renderer types (RenderCell, RenderGrid, ScreenModel, ViewModel). Imports from both vim_rogue::types and vim_rogue::render_types to test the extracted module separation.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Root architecture documentation with 13 sections covering structure, architecture, where to look, conventions, commands, verification checklist, dependencies, notes, and agent skills. Updated to reflect render_types.rs module extraction.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Source code architecture documentation with 7 sections covering key files, where to look, internal dependencies, conventions, tests, and notes. Updated to include render_types.rs in the module list and dependency graph.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Test architecture documentation with 7 sections covering test files, shared helpers, test constructor, where to look, conventions, and notes. References render_types import from lib.rs.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project README with 10 sections covering features, Vim motions, quick start, controls, build & test, architecture (updated with render_types.rs), key design decisions, dependencies, and license.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Changelog with 20 sections tracking version history. Merged Unreleased into v0.3.0 documenting the render_types.rs extraction and removal of renderer types from types.rs.

nodes.name: ONBOARDING.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive onboarding guide with 34 sections covering architecture layers, key concepts (aggregate pattern, deterministic timing, renderer isolation), guided tour through all source files, file map, complexity hotspots, development workflow, and conventions. Generated from knowledge graph analysis.

nodes.name: World
nodes.summary: Primary aggregate owning map, visibility, enemies, and activated torchlights. Provides enemy stepping, collision resolution, and visibility updates. Coordinates map data with enemy AI and FOV systems.

nodes.name: PlayerState
nodes.summary: Player aggregate owning position, motion tracking (used_motions, discovered_motions, motion_count), checkpoint system, noclip mode, HP, and trail. Handles level transitions and retry logic.

nodes.name: InputState
nodes.summary: Input aggregate managing input queue for buffered keypresses and pending input state for two-phase motions (f/t/dd/gg). Supports input queueing and pending motion tracking.

nodes.name: Session
nodes.summary: Session aggregate tracking game state (Playing/Won/Dying/Lost/Paused/Quit), pause menu selection, started flag, status message, and timing (start time, elapsed, final time). Manages game flow and session persistence.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root application coordinator assembling all aggregates (world, player, input, session) with player animation, enemy animations, attack effects, audio manager, and cheat buffer. Thin coordinator that delegates to aggregates.

nodes.name: VimMotion
nodes.summary: Enum of 13 Vim motions (h/j/k/l/w/b/0/$/f/t/dd/g) with key_label, display_name, and description methods. Provides motion metadata for UI display and teaching.

nodes.name: Tile
nodes.summary: Tile type enum (Wall/Floor/Exit/Obstacle/Torchlight) with glyph and Display formatting. Represents the 5 possible tile states in the dungeon grid.

nodes.name: Enemy
nodes.summary: Enemy entity with position, glyph, optional HP (level 4 enemies have 30 HP), stunned turn counter, and patrol area. Supports both patrol-only and FOV-aware chase behaviors.

nodes.name: PatrolArea
nodes.summary: Axis-aligned bounding box for enemy patrol areas defined by min/max x/y coordinates. Provides point factory and containment testing for room-based enemy AI.

nodes.name: RenderCell
nodes.summary: Renderer cell type with glyph, foreground RGB, background RGB, and optional blink. Provides builder-style new() and with_blink() methods for terminal rendering.

nodes.name: RenderGrid
nodes.summary: 2D grid of RenderCells with width, height, and cells vector. Provides new(), width(), height(), get(x,y), set(x,y,cell), and fill(cell) methods for terminal framebuffer operations.

nodes.name: ViewModel
nodes.summary: View state wrapping ScreenModel (Title/Gameplay/Win/Lost) with frame_number counter for animation. Provides new() and advance_frame() for frame-based rendering.

nodes.name: ScreenModel
nodes.summary: Screen state enumeration (Title/Gameplay/Win/Lost) representing the 4 possible rendering modes. Maps game states to rendering strategies.

nodes.name: World::step_enemies
nodes.summary: Core enemy turn orchestration: captures prior positions, steps each enemy (FOV chase or patrol), builds movement/collision results, and returns EnemyTurn with movements and collision indices. Delegates AI to enemy.rs methods.

nodes.name: World::push_enemies_off_position
nodes.summary: BFS-based enemy collision resolution that finds nearest empty tiles for enemies occupying a position, respecting passability and avoiding other enemy positions. Uses visited/claimed sets for pathfinding.

nodes.name: World::update_visibility
nodes.summary: Updates FOV visibility map: demotes visible to explored, computes player FOV, computes activated torchlight FOVs with TORCHLIGHT_FOV_RADIUS. Supports multi-source FOV for checkpoint system.

nodes.name: PlayerState::advance_level
nodes.summary: Increments player level, clears trail, and sets starting position for level transition. Used when player reaches the exit tile.

nodes.name: PlayerState::retry_level
nodes.summary: Maintains current level, clears trail, and sets starting position for retry after death. Used when player loses all lives or selects retry from pause menu.

nodes.name: RenderGrid::fill
nodes.summary: Fills entire grid with cloned RenderCell by iterating all cells. Used for clearing screen or setting uniform background.

nodes.name: ViewModel::advance_frame
nodes.summary: Increments frame_number counter for animation sequencing. Called each render cycle to drive interpolated animations.

layers.name: Domain Core
layers.description: Core domain types and data structures defining Position, Tile, Zone, VimMotion, Direction, GameState, enemy types, and domain aggregates (World, PlayerState, InputState, Session, App). The foundational type system for the entire game.

layers.name: Game Logic
layers.description: Core game systems including map generation (80x40 grid, 4 levels, procedural carving), player movement (13 Vim motions with boundary checking), enemy AI (FOV-aware BFS pathfinding and patrol), and visibility system (ray-casting FOV with fog-of-war persistence).

layers.name: Application
layers.description: App coordinator orchestrating the game loop: input processing via handle_key, animation updates via tick, motion execution via execute_motion, enemy turn orchestration via enemies_step, level transitions, pause menu, and game state machine management.

layers.name: Presentation
layers.description: Pure rendering layer using bracket-lib to draw title/gameplay/win/lost/pause screens, viewport with FOV fog, sidebar with stats, minimap, zone-colored walls, animation effects, and renderer-specific types (RenderCell, RenderGrid, ScreenModel, ViewModel). Read-only with no state mutation.

layers.name: System Support
layers.description: Cross-cutting concerns including animation timing system (GameClock trait with RealClock/TestClock, AnimationTimer, Interpolator easing functions, AttackEffect combat visuals) and audio manager (SoundEffect enum with enable/disable toggle and graceful no-op fallback).

layers.name: Entry Points
layers.description: Binary entry point (main.rs initializing bracket-lib console and event loop) and library root (lib.rs re-exporting all modules including render_types) providing the public API surface.

layers.name: Integration Tests
layers.description: 396 comprehensive integration tests across 9 files covering all 13 Vim motions, game state machine transitions, map structure validation, rendering output, animation timing, FOV behavior, enemy AI pathfinding, audio system, and domain types. Includes shared test helpers (test_map, tick_timer, approx_eq) and test seam (App::for_test).

layers.name: Examples
layers.description: Proof-of-concept spike demonstrating bracket-lib basics including console creation, character drawing, background colors, and input handling.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project configuration files including Rust package manifest (Cargo.toml with edition 2024 and bracket-lib dependency), cross-compilation settings (Cross.toml for Linux x86_64/ARM64), code formatter configuration (rustfmt.toml with small heuristics), and Git blame ignore rules.

layers.name: CI/CD
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows for continuous integration (ci.yml running format check, clippy linting, testing across 6 platforms, cross-compilation builds) and release automation (release.yml building cross-platform binaries and publishing GitHub releases with auto-extracted changelog).

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation including root architecture (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CONTEXT.md with DDD aggregates), source code guides (src/AGENTS.md, src/CLAUDE.md), test documentation (tests/CLAUDE.md, tests/AGENTS.md), user-facing content (README.md with features/controls, CHANGELOG.md with version history), onboarding guide (docs/ONBOARDING.md), agent workflows (docs/agents/), and specifications (docs/specs/aggregate-refactor.md).

layers.name: Test Support
layers.description: Test infrastructure providing App::for_test constructor for deterministic test setup with custom map and position, used by all 396 integration tests.

tour.title: Entry Point and Library Structure
tour.description: The journey begins at the binary entry point (main.rs) which initializes bracket-lib console and sets up the main event loop. It delegates to lib.rs, a module barrel file that re-exports all game modules including the new render_types module, establishing the public API surface. This thin ~44-line bootstrap demonstrates Rust's binary/library separation pattern.
tour.languageLesson: Rust binaries use src/main.rs as the entry point, while libraries use src/lib.rs for their public API. The lib.rs barrel file pattern uses 'pub mod' and 'pub use' to re-export modules, creating a clean public API surface that abstracts internal structure.

tour.title: Core Domain Types and Aggregates
tour.description: The foundational type system lives in types.rs, the most depended-upon module (12 imports). It defines Position, Tile, Zone, VimMotion, Direction, GameState, enemy types, and four key DDD aggregates: World (enemies + visibility + map), PlayerState (position + motion tracking + checkpoint), InputState (input queue), and Session (game state + timing). This is the vocabulary used throughout the entire codebase.
tour.languageLesson: Rust's type system enables domain-driven design through lightweight enums and structs. The aggregate pattern groups related state and behavior: World owns step_enemies + push_enemies_off_position, PlayerState owns motion tracking, keeping domains separate and cohesive.

tour.title: Presentation Layer Types
tour.description: Renderer-specific types are extracted into render_types.rs, maintaining the critical separation between domain logic and presentation. This module defines RenderCell (glyph + colors + blink), RenderGrid (2D grid with width/height), ScreenModel (Title/Gameplay/Win/Lost states), and ViewModel (screen + frame counter). The renderer.rs module uses these types exclusively, never touching domain aggregates directly.
tour.languageLesson: Separating presentation types from domain types prevents renderer concerns from leaking into business logic. RenderCell, RenderGrid, and ViewModel are pure data structures for display, while Tile, Position, and VimMotion represent the game domain. This isolation makes the renderer read-only and easily testable.

tour.title: Map Generation and Structure
tour.description: The 80x40 grid map with 5 procedural zones comes from map.rs, a highly depended-upon module (9 imports). It implements corridor carving, builds 4 distinct dungeon levels (basic, inverted maze + obstacles, zigzag + BFS enemies, fortress rooms + FOV patrol), places enemy spawn points, defines patrol areas, and positions torchlights. The row-major grid[y][x] convention is used throughout.
tour.languageLesson: Procedural generation uses room carving algorithms: start with solid walls, carve rooms, connect with corridors, then spawn entities. The 'is_passable' predicate determines which tiles (Floor, Exit, Torchlight) allow movement, while Obstacle blocks but can be destroyed by dd. Zone assignments (5 total) enable colored wall rendering.

tour.title: Player Movement and Vim Motions
tour.description: PlayerState in player.rs implements all 13 Vim motion handlers: h/j/k/l (cardinal), w/b (word jumps), 0/$ (line ends), G/gg (vertical scan to bounds), f/t (find/till pending), and dd (destroy obstacle). Each handler performs boundary checking, wall stopping logic, and motion tracking (motion_count increment, discovered_motions set). The handle_motion method takes &mut Map since dd deletes obstacles.
tour.languageLesson: Rust pattern matching on enums makes Vim motion dispatch elegant. Pending input state (f/t/dd/gg) stores the first key in InputState, awaiting the second keystroke. This two-phase input pattern is common in text editors and requires careful state machine management.

tour.title: Enemy AI and Pathfinding
tour.description: Enemy intelligence in enemy.rs uses FOV-aware BFS pathfinding for chase (step_toward_player) with Bresenham line-of-sight detection (has_line_of_sight), plus patrol movement within assigned PatrolArea when the player isn't visible. The World aggregate's step_enemies method orchestrates all enemy turns, handling collision outcomes and movement animations. This creates the game's dynamic threat response.
tour.languageLesson: BFS (Breadth-First Search) guarantees shortest pathfinding in unweighted grids. The algorithm explores outward from the enemy until reaching the player, building a path backwards. Bresenham's line algorithm efficiently checks line-of-sight without floating-point math by walking the grid with integer error accumulation.

tour.title: Visibility and Fog of War
tour.description: The FOV system in visibility.rs uses ray-casting to compute visible tiles from the player position, with three visibility states: Hidden (unseen), Visible (currently in FOV), and Explored (seen before but not currently). The World aggregate's update_visibility method computes the FOV radius each turn. Explored areas persist across updates, creating the classic fog-of-war effect that rewards map memory.
tour.languageLesson: Ray-casting FOV sends rays in all directions (360 degrees) from the player, stopping at walls. Each visited tile becomes Visible; previously Visible tiles become Explored. This is more efficient than shadow-casting for simple roguelikes and produces natural circular visibility with proper wall occlusion.

tour.title: Application Coordinator and Game Loop
tour.description: The App coordinator in game.rs orchestrates the entire game loop with 16 dependencies. It implements handle_key for input processing (two-phase for f/t/dd/gg), tick for animation updates, execute_motion for player movement triggering enemy turns, enemies_step for turn orchestration, and level transitions. This is the central traffic controller that sequences cross-aggregate flows while keeping each aggregate focused on its domain.
tour.languageLesson: The coordinator pattern delegates to domain experts: execute_motion calls PlayerState::handle_motion, which may mutate the Map; enemies_step calls World::step_enemies, which iterates Enemy::step_toward_player. Each aggregate owns its state (World owns enemies, PlayerState owns position), and App sequences the interactions.

tour.title: Animation and Timing System
tour.description: Smooth animations rely on the animation.rs module with its GameClock trait (RealClock for production, TestClock for deterministic tests), AnimationState for transitions, AnimationTimer for duration tracking, Interpolator for easing functions (linear, ease-in, ease-out, ease-in-out), and AttackEffect for combat visuals. The tick method advances animations by delta milliseconds, making the game feel responsive and polished.
tour.languageLesson: Trait-based time abstraction (GameClock) enables deterministic testing: TestClock returns fixed values, while RealClock uses std::time::Instant. Easing functions implement interpolation formulas like t*t for ease-in quadratic acceleration. The AnimationTimer tracks progress (0.0 to 1.0) over a duration, triggering phase transitions.

tour.title: Audio System
tour.description: The audio.rs module provides a SoundEffect enum for all game events and an AudioManager with enable/disable toggle. Audio is off by default, with graceful no-op fallback when disabled—no sound plays, but the game logic continues uninterrupted. This design keeps audio optional without complicating the core game systems, making it easy to add sound later without refactoring.
tour.languageLesson: The null object pattern makes optional dependencies painless: AudioManager::play is a no-op when disabled, so callers don't need conditional logic. The SoundEffect enum exhaustively lists all audio events, providing a single place to add new sounds. This separation keeps audio concerns isolated from core gameplay.

tour.title: Test Infrastructure and Deterministic Setup
tour.description: The test_support.rs module provides App::for_test, a test seam constructor that creates an App with a custom map and position. All 396 integration tests across 9 files use this constructor for deterministic setup. The shared test helpers in tests/common/mod.rs provide test_map, tick_timer, tick_state, approx_eq, and FOV testing utilities. This infrastructure makes tests fast, reliable, and easy to write.
tour.languageLesson: Test seam constructors separate production logic from test setup. App::for_test bypasses random map generation, accepting a fixed map and position. This makes tests deterministic and fast—no need to fight randomness. Shared test helpers (test_map, tick_timer) reduce duplication across 396 tests, enforcing consistency.

tour.title: Project Configuration and Build System
tour.description: The Cargo.toml manifest defines the vim-rogue 0.2.2 package with Rust edition 2024 and bracket-lib 0.8.7 dependency. Cross.toml configures cross-compilation for Linux x86_64 and ARM64. rustfmt.toml enforces code style with small heuristics. These configuration files control how the project builds, runs on different platforms, and maintains consistent formatting across contributors.
tour.languageLesson: Cargo.toml is the single source of truth for dependencies, package metadata, and build configuration. Edition 2024 enables modern Rust features. Cross.toml extends cargo with cross-rs for cross-compilation without local toolchains. rustfmt.toml's 'use_small_heuristics = Max' prefers shorter lines within the default width limit.

tour.title: CI/CD Pipeline and Release Automation
tour.description: GitHub Actions workflows automate quality and releases. ci.yml runs format checks, clippy linting, tests across 6 platforms, and cross-compilation builds on every push. release.yml triggers on version tags, builds cross-platform binaries, and publishes GitHub releases with auto-extracted changelog. This automation ensures the project always ships tested, working code across all supported platforms.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions uses workflow YAML with 'on' triggers for push/tags, 'jobs' for parallel tasks, and 'steps' for sequential commands. Matrix builds test across multiple OS/language versions (6 platforms here). The cargo-release tool extracts CHANGELOG.md entries for release notes, automating the release process while keeping documentation in sync.

tour.title: Documentation and Onboarding
tour.description: The project documentation ecosystem includes AGENTS.md (root architecture), CLAUDE.md (project overview), CONTEXT.md (DDD aggregates and domain rules), README.md (user-facing features and controls), CHANGELOG.md (version history), and docs/ONBOARDING.md (comprehensive 34-section guide). These documents explain architecture, conventions, commands, verification checklist, and development workflow, enabling smooth contributor onboarding.
tour.languageLesson: Good documentation lives alongside code. AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md provide architecture and conventions for AI-assisted development. CONTEXT.md defines the domain language (aggregates, rules, naming). README.md is the user's first impression. ONBOARDING.md synthesizes everything into a guided narrative. Keeping docs in version control ensures they evolve with the codebase.

tour.title: Comprehensive Test Coverage
tour.description: The project boasts 396 integration tests across 9 files, validating every aspect: all 13 Vim motions (player.rs tests), game state machine transitions (game.rs tests, 140 tests alone), map structure and reachability (map.rs tests), rendering output and zone colors (renderer.rs tests), animation timing and easing (animation.rs tests), FOV behavior and explored state persistence (visibility.rs tests), enemy BFS pathfinding (enemy.rs tests), and audio system (audio.rs tests). This coverage ensures confidence in refactoring and prevents regressions.
tour.languageLesson: Integration tests in Rust go in the tests/ directory, separate from src/. They import the library crate (vim_rogue) and test public APIs. The test constructor pattern (App::for_test) provides deterministic setup. High coverage doesn't guarantee bug-free code, but it does prevent regressions and documents expected behavior. Each test file focuses on one module, keeping tests organized and readable.
